nodes.title: Applied Systems Pharmacology
nodes.description: A systems approach to basic and applied pharmacology, including pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic principles, drug receptors and mechanisms, and clinically relevant adverse effects. Develops skills in diagnostic reasoning and evidence-based medicine. Prerequisite: Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student or Instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override. Must have a strong background in biology and chemistry.

nodes.title: Medical Cannabis
nodes.description: An introduction to the pharmacology underlying recreational and medicinal uses of Cannabis. Focuses on Cannabis taxonomy, chemistry of cannabinoids, physiological effects, and emerging therapeutic applications. Discusses historical, political and socio-economic influences on medical marijuana legislation. Prerequisite: Introductory Biology and Chemistry courses; organic chemistry or PHRM 3010 recommended.
nodes.other_requirements: Background in biology and chemistry, or permission required; Degree students only even after level restriction removed; PACE students register for PHRM 3000 ZRA; Colocated with PHRM 3000 ZRA

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Graduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Medical Master's Capstone
nodes.description: Students advance their fundamental knowledge in Biochemistry, Pharmacology, and Physiology by addressing therapeutic applications in a discussion format. Students will choose and research current clinical problems and will communicate new molecular strategies through formal presentations. Prerequisites: BIOC 6001, MPBP 6010; Medical Science Graduate student; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Medical Science Graduate student; or Instructor permission. Restricted to Medical Sciences Masters Students

nodes.title: Molecules & Medicine
nodes.description: Conveys the molecular mechanisms by which drugs act in the body and the principles of drug design. Highlights the importance of medicinal chemistry as it overlaps with the disciplines of Chemistry, Biochemistry, Microbiology, Cell Biology, and Pharmacology. Prerequisites: Organic Chemistry and Biology, Biochemistry, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override. This course is appropriate for UG students; no special permission is required. 5000 level courses are open to Undergraduate and Graduate Students.

nodes.title: Medical Cell Biology
nodes.description: Explores the structure and function of eukaryotic cells in multicellular organisms with a special emphasis on the human model. Appropriate for graduate students who have a strong background in biology and chemistry and students with an interest in pursuing health-related fields. Prerequisite: Graduate student, AMP student, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student, AMP student, or Instructor permission

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for the Doctoral Dissertation.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission is required.

nodes.title: Milestones in Pharmacology
nodes.description: A critical readings class where students read and present landmark pharmacology papers and link them to modern experiments and clinical applications. Co-requisite: PHRM 3010 or Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student, PACE students by permission and override; PACE students require permission

nodes.title: Gr Toxicology
nodes.description: Provides an understanding of the chemical, biochemical, and physiological factors that determine the pathological effects of chemicals in living systems. Explores the principles of toxicology, focusing on molecular, environmental, and clinical aspects of toxins. Topics include metabolic pathways, toxicogenomics, environmental and plant toxins, chemical and biological warfare agents, heavy metals, and forensic toxicology. Prerequisite: Introductory Biology, Organic Chemistry, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission, PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Co-requisite: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Pharmacology and Therapeutics
nodes.description: Focuses on biochemical and physiological actions of prototype drugs used in the treatment and prevention of human diseases. Prerequisite: Introductory courses in Biology and Chemistry. Pre/Co-requisite: Organic chemistry recommended.
nodes.other_requirements: Introductory courses in Biology and Chemistry. Organic chemistry recommended. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Undergrad Res - Honors Thesis
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Prerequisite: PHRM 3010. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Offered at department discretion. Instructor Permission required; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for PHRM 3995 ZRA; Colocated with PHRM 3995 ZRA; Total combined enrollment: 10;

nodes.title: Readings in Pharmacology
nodes.description: Intensive directed reading in one area of Pharmacology. Pharmacology students must choose a topic outside thesis research area. Term paper and seminar on selected topic required. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Human Biology w/lab
nodes.description: For nonscience majors. Selected biological concepts and topics relevant to humans, such as cancer, human genetics, environmental toxicants. With virtual laboratory. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1100 and BIOL 1105.

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience w/lab
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. With lab. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Pre/Co-requisites: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. Must register for a lab: NSCI 2100 A01-A03. For lecture only course, see NSCI 2105. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Integrative Physiol. & Pharm.
nodes.description: Intended for students pursuing careers in basic scientific research or health-related fields, designed to combine general physiological principles with examples of disease-based pathophysiology and targeted pharmacological approaches. Case studies will emphasize the impact of these processes on human function. Pre/Co-requisites: Two semesters of Chemistry, two semesters of Physics,  a background in Biology/Physiology or Health Sciences.
nodes.prereq_expression: {"type": "AND", "operands": [{"type": "course", "code": "CHEM X000"}, {"type": "course", "code": "CHEM X000"}, {"type": "course", "code": "PHYS X000"}, {"type": "course", "code": "PHYS X000"}, {"type": "OR", "operands": [{"type": "course", "code": "BIOL X000"}, {"type": "course", "code": "PHYSIOL X000"}, {"type": "course", "code": "HEALTH SCI X000"}]}]}
nodes.other_requirements: Two semesters of Chemistry, two semesters of Physics, a background in Biology/Physiology or Health Sciences. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission is required.

nodes.title: Honors Thesis - Pharmacology
nodes.description: See schedule of courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Gr Adv Pharmacology Topics
nodes.description: Focuses on basic pharmacological principles, drug interactions with receptors, membranes, synapses, neurotransmitters, macromolecules, ion channels, the cytoskeleton, and membrane pumps. Recent studies of the molecular and cellular mechanisms of drug action are discussed, and state-of-the-art techniques for pharmacological analysis of various cellular target molecules are described. Credit not awarded for both PHRM 5900 and PHRM 3900. Prerequisite: Introductory Biology or Biochemistry or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Pharmacological Techniques
nodes.description: Experiments conducted under supervision in the areas of drug metabolism, modes of drug action, physicochemical properties of drugs, bioassay, and toxicology. Thesis Master's students limited to three credits.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override; Instructor permission required Research experience for graduate students looking for technical experience or working on research for a thesis. Students should contact the researcher directly to discuss opportunities in their lab. Permission is required for enrollment.

nodes.title: Seminar
nodes.description: Current developments in Pharmacology are presented for discussion by students. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: General Biochemistry I
nodes.description: Survey for science majors. Chemistry, structure, metabolism, and function of proteins, carbohydrates, lipids; enzymes, bioenergetics and respiratory processes. Prerequisite: CHEM 2585 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE & Post Bac Pre Med students register for BIOC 6001 ZRA; Total combined enrollment = 50; There is also a Wed. class 4-6 p.m. in Given C443

nodes.title: Human Physiology & Pharm I
nodes.description: An integrated examination of the physiology and pharmacology of the peripheral nervous, muscle and cardiovascular systems in the human body. Pre/co-requisites: CHEM 1450, CHEM 1580 or equivalent; two semesters general physics; two semesters calculus. May not be taken for credit with MPBP 6060.
nodes.other_requirements: two semesters general physics; two semesters calculus. May not be taken for credit with MPBP 6060. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: IS:Capstone Research in Pharm
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Molecular & Cell Biology w/lab
nodes.description: Explores the fundamental processes of life. Topics include cellular metabolism; structure and function of organelles; cell cycle; signal transduction; biology of cancer. CHEM 2580, BCOR 2300 recommended. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 2500 and BCOR 2505. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; CHEM 1400 or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For majors: ASCI, BICM, BIOL, BISC, ENSC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL only. Minimum sophomore standing. If enrolling in CHEM 1450/1460, please request an override from Laura.A.Newman@uvm.edu. Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med students enroll in BCOR 2500 ZRA.
